summary: In this session, student_c sought last-minute study strategies with only two hours left before the exam. The assistant advised focusing on high-yield topics, specifically Trace reasoning (25 marks), Fault analysis (15 marks), and Consensus protocols (15 marks). The user was encouraged to review specific past exam traces and to practice distinguishing between thin and fabricated evidence in Fault analysis. For Evidence naming, the assistant emphasized the importance of clarity and specificity in answers. Student_c also received tips on managing exam time effectively, suggesting starting with questions they felt most confident about and allocating time based on the marks for each section. The user expressed a desire to memorize key terms relevant to the exam, which the assistant provided. Overall, student_c felt prepared with a solid strategy, reinforcing their understanding of previously discussed concepts and focusing on high-yield areas for their final review.
key_facts: Student_c should focus on Trace reasoning, Fault analysis, and Consensus protocols for the exam.
Specific past exam traces A, B, and D were recommended for review.
Thin evidence can support a claim but lacks depth, while fabricated evidence is misleading.
Clarity and specificity are crucial for scoring well in Evidence naming.
Time management strategies include starting with confident questions and allocating time based on marks.
positions_taken: Student_c feels confident in their exam strategy.
The user intends to practice distinguishing between types of evidence.
